queries.name: findDocByOrg
queries.sourceSql:
-- Single-statement file with a `-- name:` override: the path default would be
-- docLookup, but the marker renames it verbatim to findDocByOrg.
-- name: findDocByOrg
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Single-statement file with a `-- name:` override: the path default would be
-- docLookup, but the marker renames it verbatim to findDocByOrg.
-- name: findDocByOrg
SELECT id, body
FROM docs
WHERE org_id = :orgId
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, body FROM docs WHERE org_id = ? ORDER BY id

queries.name: docWithBody
queries.sourceSql:
-- Join across docs+tasks (same project); projection mixes both tables.
SELECT d.id AS doc_id, d.body, t.title AS task_title
FROM docs d
JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id
WHERE d.org_id = :orgId
queries.sql:
-- Join across docs+tasks (same project); projection mixes both tables.
SELECT d.id AS docId, d.body, t.title AS taskTitle FROM docs d
JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id
WHERE d.org_id = :orgId
queries.positionalSql: SELECT d.id AS docId, d.body, t.title AS taskTitle FROM docs d JOIN tasks t ON t.project_id = d.project_id WHERE d.org_id = ?

queries.name: listProjectTasks
queries.sourceSql:
-- Plain select + param inference: :projectId types to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done, priority, estimate
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY priority
queries.sql:
-- Plain select + param inference: :projectId types to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done, priority, estimate
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId
ORDER BY priority
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title, done, priority, estimate FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY priority

queries.name: projectDocCount
queries.sourceSql:
-- Aggregate (count(*) → fallback nullable number) + a plain grouped ref.
SELECT project_id, count(*) AS doc_count
FROM docs
GROUP BY project_id
queries.sql:
-- Aggregate (count(*) → fallback nullable number) + a plain grouped ref.
SELECT project_id AS projectId, count(*) AS docCount FROM docs
GROUP BY project_id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT project_id AS projectId, count(*) AS docCount FROM docs GROUP BY project_id

queries.name: reportingTasksByPriority
queries.sourceSql:
-- Nested-folder query: default name is path-derived
-- (reporting/tasks-by-priority.sql → reportingTasksByPriority).
SELECT id, title, priority
FROM tasks
WHERE priority = :priority
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Nested-folder query: default name is path-derived
-- (reporting/tasks-by-priority.sql → reportingTasksByPriority).
SELECT id, title, priority
FROM tasks
WHERE priority = :priority
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title, priority FROM tasks WHERE priority = ? ORDER BY id

queries.name: reportOpenTasks
queries.sourceSql:
-- Multi-statement file: two queries in one .sql. Each statement REQUIRES a
-- `-- name:` marker (the file's path can't disambiguate them) and each carries
-- its own leading `-- param`/`-- name` scope.

-- name: reportOpenTasks
-- One statement's scope: infers :projectId from project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId AND done = 0
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- Multi-statement file: two queries in one .sql. Each statement REQUIRES a
-- `-- name:` marker (the file's path can't disambiguate them) and each carries
-- its own leading `-- param`/`-- name` scope.

-- name: reportOpenTasks
-- One statement's scope: infers :projectId from project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title, done
FROM tasks
WHERE project_id = :projectId AND done = 0
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title, done FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? AND done = 0 ORDER BY id

queries.name: reportDocScores
queries.sourceSql:
-- name: reportDocScores
-- A separate statement, separately scoped: :minScore is compared to an
-- expression, so it needs its own `-- param` comment.
-- param :minScore float
SELECT id, org_id, score
FROM docs
WHERE score > :minScore * 1.0
ORDER BY id
queries.sql:
-- name: reportDocScores
-- A separate statement, separately scoped: :minScore is compared to an
-- expression, so it needs its own `-- param` comment.
-- param :minScore float
SELECT id, org_id AS orgId, score FROM docs
WHERE score > :minScore * 1.0
ORDER BY id
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, org_id AS orgId, score FROM docs WHERE score > ? * 1.0 ORDER BY id

queries.name: searchTasks
queries.sourceSql:
select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at
  from tasks
  where @inProject(:projectId)
    and @search(:q)
    and priority >= :minPriority
    and if (:openOnly) { done = 0 }
queries.sql: select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks where (project_id = :projectId) and (:q is null or ((title like '%' || :q || '%'))) and (:minPriority is null or (priority >= :minPriority)) and (coalesce(:openOnly, 0) = 0 or (done = 0)) order by priority desc limit min(coalesce(:limit, 50), 200)
queries.positionalSql: select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks where (project_id = ?1) and (?2 is null or ((title like '%' || ?2 || '%'))) and (?3 is null or (priority >= ?3)) and (coalesce(?4, 0) = 0 or (done = 0)) order by priority desc limit min(coalesce(?5, 50), 200)
queries.positionalSqlBase: select id, title, done, priority, estimated_at AS estimatedAt from tasks where (project_id = ?1) and (?2 is null or ((title like '%' || ?2 || '%'))) and (?3 is null or (priority >= ?3)) and (coalesce(?4, 0) = 0 or (done = 0))

queries.name: taskEstimateRange
queries.sourceSql:
select id, title, estimate
  from tasks
  where @inProject(:projectId)
    and estimated_at between :from and :to
queries.sql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = :projectId) and (:from is null or :to is null or (estimated_at between :from and :to))
queries.positionalSql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = ?1) and (?2 is null or ?3 is null or (estimated_at between ?2 and ?3))

variants.sql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = :projectId)
variants.positionalSql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = ?)

variants.sql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = :projectId) and estimated_at between :from and :to
variants.positionalSql: select id, title, estimate from tasks where (project_id = ?) and estimated_at between ? and ?

queries.name: taskTitles
queries.sourceSql:
-- Projection subset (a narrow row), no params.
SELECT id, title FROM tasks
queries.sql:
-- Projection subset (a narrow row), no params.
SELECT id, title FROM tasks

queries.name: tasksInProjects
queries.sourceSql:
-- IN-clause param inference: both params type to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE project_id IN (:first, :second)
queries.sql:
-- IN-clause param inference: both params type to project_id (TEXT).
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE project_id IN (:first, :second)
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE project_id IN (?, ?)

queries.name: tasksSince
queries.sourceSql:
-- Comment-typed param: :sinceMs isn't compared to a plain column (it hits an
-- expression), so its type is declared explicitly.
-- param :sinceMs integer
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > :sinceMs + 0
queries.sql:
-- Comment-typed param: :sinceMs isn't compared to a plain column (it hits an
-- expression), so its type is declared explicitly.
-- param :sinceMs integer
SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > :sinceMs + 0
queries.positionalSql: SELECT id, title FROM tasks WHERE estimated_at > ? + 0
